message:
fix(frankensqlite): include underscore in word boundary check for SQL keyword parsing

The find_keyword_at_depth_zero function was incorrectly matching SQL keywords
inside identifiers containing underscores. For example, "from_name" would match
"FROM" because underscore wasn't considered part of the word.

This fixes column alias parsing for queries like:
SELECT a.name as from_name FROM table

Before: Parsed as "name as" (incorrect - FROM matched inside from_name)
After: Parsed as "from_name" (correct)

Fixes 14+ test failures in mcp-agent-mail-db related to column name inference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
